title: F012 — Observation Collapse

requiredObjects: central registry or shared protocol layer
central symbolic heart or integration point

composition.colorLogic: cool scientific background, bright path lines, restrained accent colors
composition.labelPolicy: minimal labels, no dense paragraphs inside image

qaChecklist: Avoids forbidden objects and fake text
Does not overuse mystical or decorative elements

prompts.providerAgnostic:
Create a publication-quality scientific book figure titled "F012 — Observation Collapse".
Purpose: Potential states narrow into an experienced outcome through measurement or observation, with careful distinction between physics and metaphysical interpretation.

Manuscript context: One of the most debated questions in physics concerns what occurs during measurement. Prior to observation, quantum systems are represented by multiple possibilities. After observation, only one outcome appears. This transition is commonly described as wave function collapse. The exact nature of this process remains an active area of philosophical and scientific discussion. The FHQCM does not claim to solve the measurement problem. Instead, it treats measurement as evidence that reality contains relational dynamics that cannot be fully described by classical materialism alone. F012 — Observation CollapsePotential states narrow into an experienced outcome through measurement or observation, with careful distinction between physics and metaphysical interpretation.F012-observation-collapse John Wheeler and Participatory Reality Physicist John Archibald Wheeler proposed one of the most intriguing interpretations of quantum observation. Wheeler suggested that the universe may be participatory in nature. His famous phrase ``It from Bit'' proposed that information occupies a foundational role in reality. According to Wheeler, physical phenomena may emerge from acts of distinction, measurement, and information acquisition. The observer is not necessarily external to reality.

Scientific/conceptual concepts: wave-particle relationship, quantum state transition, delayed-choice measurement, observer-dependent experimental arrangement, observer participation, federated systems architecture, symbolic integration.
Required visual objects: wavefront or probability field, measurement boundary, photon path, beam splitter or choice point, detector A, detector B, observer or measurement station, observer axis or observation node, information flow arrows, nodes, links, central registry or shared protocol layer, central symbolic heart or integration point, spiral or healing path.
Recommended short labels: superposition, measurement, choice point, detector, observer, information.

Composition: left-to-right experimental flow.
Camera/view: orthographic.
Visual hierarchy: source/emission → path or choice point → measurement/detector → observer/information interpretation.
Color logic: cool scientific background, bright path lines, restrained accent colors.
Label policy: minimal labels, no dense paragraphs inside image.

Avoid: fake citations, fake equations, unreadable text, watermarks, copyrighted characters, random mystical clutter, misleading scientific labels, random galaxies unless explicitly required, decorative clutter.

Make it clean, serious, elegant, uncluttered, and suitable for an academic manuscript.
